title: Determinants of Advanced Countries — Yahir's development checklist
html:
TL;DR

A 16-question rubric drafted Aug 31 2024 for ranking how "advanced" a country actually is — clearly written from inside china during the china chapter, where the pattern-matching against the U.S. is happening in real time. Mixes plumbing-grade infra questions with social/aesthetic ones in a way that's revealing about what Yahir actually cares about.

The questions

Infrastructure

  • Can you flush toilet paper?
  • Is there good AC throughout?
  • Is there a way to dry hands?
  • How many faucets turn (push/lever/sensor)?
  • Can you shower without sealing your mouth shut from the water?
  • Do they accept card throughout?
  • What does the airport security look like?

Social / cultural

  • How is there English (availability)?
  • What do their mini-markets look like?
  • How civilized are the kids?
  • "Are there vulture women?" (aggressive solicitation)
  • Is there an opportunity cost for leisure? (do people treat downtime as a luxury or default?)
  • Do they prioritize lifting / fitness?
  • How many museums do they have?
  • Are the women outwardly stylish?

Why it matters in the graph

  • Names the markers of development concept — Yahir's personal framework for ranking countries on a felt-experience scale, not a GDP scale.
  • Sister doc to non negotiables for traveling from June 2024 — together they form the pre/early-china chapter travel epistemology.
  • The "do they prioritize lifting/fitness" line connects to the supplement stack he'd append to 2024 08 14 travel occurrences just two weeks earlier.
  • Conceptually adjacent to the trade/economics thinking in intra industry trade but operating at the lived-experience layer — what countries feel developed, not what they trade.

Open questions

  • Where does china score on his own list? The note is almost certainly a delta-from-Shanghai exercise but he doesn't say.
  • Does he revisit this rubric anywhere in 2025?
